summary: Validate R0 recovery baseline exit gate
acceptance_criteria: Verify origin/main, RR0 branch, status files, task graph, thread registry, goal-mode state, and model-routing state are internally consistent.
Verify all production entrypoints, fixture/test paths, and no-op command surfaces from the R0 audit are recorded.
Verify no forbidden product code, dependency, content-source, CI, accepted ADR, or historical Gate file changed during R0.
Confirm Autonomous Goal Mode remains enabled and no active Human Gate exists after roadmap acceptance.
Recommend R0_GATE = PASS, REQUEST_CHANGES, or BLOCK with evidence.
required_tests: node -e parse all changed JSON files
git diff --name-only check proving no forbidden paths changed
notes: R0 exit validation must not start R1 work.
If any required check cannot run, status must be PARTIAL or BLOCKED rather than DONE.
next_action: The same systems_architect performs the R3 coordination-only repair requested by independent QA, reruns the R0 state matrix, and returns a REVIEW handoff to the same qa_reviewer.

basis: origin/main, current branch, task graph, status files, thread registry, goal-mode, model-routing, continuation, and route files are internally consistent for R0 Exit.
R0 audit evidence fully records production, fixture, test/demo, no-op, client-local success, and missing-authoritative-runtime paths without hiding the R1 gap.
No forbidden product, dependency, CI, content-source, MANIFEST/SHA, or historical Gate file changes were found in R0 scope.
No RR1 task files exist and no R1 work is authorized before QA review and Lead synchronization.
limitations: This is a process and evidence Gate; the authoritative client runtime gap remains a recorded R1 risk, not an R0 blocker.
The protected M7 renderer worktree still has 20 retained partial entries and must remain untouched until an approved follow-up task.
